metadata.title: Build an Interface-Brand Alignment Checker with Claude
metadata.note: cli-ideas Card 20 — Ch 23 (Shipit: Interface and Deployment). DOUBLE-CHECK: Bard promotional video released February 6 2023; Reuters story February 8; Alphabet lost ~$100B in market cap. Error: JWST claimed first images of exoplanet — actually first by ESO Very Large Telescope in 2004. Three misalignment types: confidence (Bard), tone (Snapchat Feb 2018 redesign — 1.2M petition signatures, Kylie Jenner tweet, stock -7%), capability (Tay March 23 2016, sixteen hours to corrupt outputs). Three alignment dimensions: voice, claim, failure state.

beats.role_note: COLD OPEN LAW — Claude UI, ask shown answered; IN-FOR-BEAR
beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Build it with Claude, then take it apart.

props.command: claude "audit interface copy against brand archetype — voice, claim, and failure state alignment"
output: Voice Alignment: PARTIAL — hero copy consistent, CTA drifts from Sage register
Claim Alignment: MISALIGNED — 'instant answers' implies certainty system cannot reliably deliver
Failure State: MISALIGNED — 'Oops!' inconsistent with Sage register; rewrite: 'Query produced no results. Narrow search term or specify date range.'

beats.role_note: stakes — BEFORE CLI loop; SHOW-DON'T-TELL: Bard loss timeline
beats.narration_text: On February sixth, 2023, Google released a promotional video for Bard. The video showed Bard answering a question about the James Webb Space Telescope with three confident bullet points. The third claimed that JWST had taken the first images of a planet outside our solar system. The first exoplanet images were actually taken by the European Southern Observatory in 2004, nearly two decades before JWST launched. Journalists noticed within hours. Reuters ran the story on February eighth. By close of trading that day, Alphabet had lost approximately one hundred billion dollars in market capitalization. The error was not the cause. The cause was the interface: a polished, Google-branded video presenting the output as confident, authoritative, Google-quality information. The interface made a promise. The system broke it in a domain where anyone with internet access could check.
beats.visual_intent: Timeline: Feb 6 2023 — Bard video released; hours later — error noticed; Feb 8 — Reuters story; trading close — '$100B market cap lost'; then three misalignment types as labeled bands: Confidence (Bard), Tone (Snapchat -7% stock), Capability (Tay, 16hrs); no composer yet

beats.role_note: ClaudeComposerAsk — show and discuss the prompt
beats.narration_text: Into Claude. Given archetype and voice adjectives, and these interface elements — hero copy, CTA text, error message, empty state, and uncertain-response text — audit across three dimensions. Dimension one: voice alignment — does the copy register match the archetype? Flag the specific word or phrase drifting furthest, and rewrite. Dimension two: claim alignment — does any copy imply a capability the tool cannot reliably fulfill? Name the claim and provide an honest version. Dimension three: failure state alignment — does the error message, empty state, and I-don't-know response hold the archetype voice under pressure, or does it collapse to generic?

props.command:
claude "audit interface-brand alignment:
  Archetype: [ARCHETYPE]
  Voice adjectives: [ADJ1, ADJ2, ADJ3]
  Interface elements:
    Hero copy: [COPY]
    CTA text: [CTA]
    Error message: [ERROR]
    Empty state: [EMPTY]
    Uncertain-response text: [UNCERTAIN]
  Three dimensions:
  1. Voice Alignment — ALIGNED/PARTIAL/MISALIGNED
     flag drifting word/phrase + rewrite
  2. Claim Alignment — any implied capability
     system cannot reliably fulfill? + honest version
  3. Failure State — error/empty/uncertain hold
     archetype voice? Rate and rewrite."

beats.role_note: ClaudeCodeBeat — actual audit script; trimmed to what teaches
beats.narration_text: Read it. The failure state check is the most-missed layer in most interface reviews. Engineers think about the interface as a presentation layer for success states. The brand check is whether the failure state — the error message, the empty state, the I-don't-know — holds the same register as the success state. A Sage archetype tool that says Oops exclamation mark when something goes wrong has a brand that collapses under pressure. The failure state is where the interface is most often honest about what was never designed.

props.title: interface_audit.py
props.code:
# interface_audit.py  —  Claude Code output
import anthropic, sys

SYSTEM = """You are an interface-brand alignment auditor.
Given: archetype, voice adjectives, interface elements.
Audit 3 dimensions:
1. Voice Alignment:
   ALIGNED | PARTIAL | MISALIGNED
   Flag specific word drifting from archetype register.
   Rewrite in correct register.
2. Claim Alignment:
   Does any copy imply capability system cannot
   reliably fulfill?
   ALIGNED | PARTIAL | MISALIGNED
   Identify claim + provide honest version.
3. Failure State Alignment:
   Does error/empty/uncertain-response hold
   archetype voice, or collapse to generic?
   Rate and rewrite each failure-state element.
For Sage: precise, explanatory, patient — not colloquial.
For Hero: decisive, direct — not apologetic.
For Caregiver: warm, forgiving — not clinical."""

client = anthropic.Anthropic()
result = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-opus-4-7", max_tokens=2048,
    system=SYSTEM,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": sys.stdin.read()}]
)
print(result.content[0].text)

beats.role_note: screen-rec or animated slot — MISALIGNED items highlighted with rewrites
beats.narration_text: Run it on a Sage-archetype tool with the error message 'Oops! Something went wrong. Try again.' Voice alignment: PARTIAL — hero copy consistent. Claim alignment: MISALIGNED — the CTA implies instant answers but the system's latency varies by query type. Failure state: MISALIGNED — 'Oops!' is flagged as inconsistent with Sage register. The rewrite prints: This query produced no results. Try narrowing the search term or specifying a date range. Precise. Explanatory. The Sage archetype held under pressure.
beats.visual_intent: screen-rec mp4: three-dimension table printing; Voice PARTIAL in amber; Claim MISALIGNED in red with original CTA highlighted and honest version beside it; Failure State MISALIGNED with 'Oops!' flagged in red and rewrite in green below; summary: 2 of 3 dimensions need fixes before deploy.

beats.narration_text: Now apply the audit to the Bard scenario to understand what the alignment check would have caught. Three confident bullet points, no source attribution, no hedging, Google quality branding. Voice: ALIGNED to Google's authority. Claim: MISALIGNED — confident assertions about JWST with no uncertainty signal in a domain where error is checkable. Failure state: not tested pre-launch. The audit would have caught claim alignment before the promotional video shipped.

props.command:
claude "audit the Bard February 2023 interface scenario:
  Archetype: Authority (Google)
  Voice: confident, precise, Google-quality
  Hero: 'Bard — your creative and helpful collaborator'
  Output format: 3 confident bullet points, no hedging
  Error state: not included in promotional video
  Uncertain-response text: none present
  Flag: which dimension would have caught the JWST error
  before the video shipped?"

beats.role_note: screen-rec or animated slot — Bard audit showing Claim MISALIGNED catching the error
beats.narration_text: The Bard audit prints: Claim Alignment MISALIGNED — the interface presents research-preview output with authoritative-product confidence. Specific element: no source attribution on factual bullet points, no 'verify with source' footer. Honest version: add source citation requirement and an 'I cannot verify this' option for low-confidence outputs. The audit catches the precise gap before the video ships. The $100 billion loss was a claim alignment problem with a known fix.
beats.visual_intent: Bard audit output: Voice ALIGNED (Google register consistent), Claim MISALIGNED (red — 'confident assertions on checkable facts, no uncertainty signal'), Failure State NOT TESTED (grey); fix panel: 'add verify-with-source footer on all factual claims'; annotation: '$100B = claim alignment failure with a known fix'

beats.narration_text: The interface is not a finishing layer. It is a promise the user re-checks every session. Three structural failure modes: confidence misalignment, capability misalignment, tone misalignment. The auditor checks all three before launch, costing almost nothing to run. The alternative cost is legible — it is approximately one hundred billion dollars and it took a promotional video about twenty-four hours to incur.
beats.visual_intent: Summary: three misalignment types as row labels; three case studies as column headers (Bard, Snapchat, Tay); grid filled with which type caused each failure; bottom: 'fix the interface — not the system; the system fix comes in the next sprint'

beats.role_note: HANDOFF LAW — prompt READ aloud AND discussed; greeting 'Your turn.'
beats.narration_text: Your turn. Pull up the actual copy from your deployed tool — hero text, CTA, error message, empty state, and any uncertain-response text you have written. Paste all of it into this prompt with your archetype and three voice adjectives. Read the failure state rating carefully. If your error message is generic — something like try again or an error occurred — you have a failure state that collapses the brand exactly when the user most needs it to hold. Rewrite those first. They are the easiest to fix and the most often left alone.

props.command:
claude "audit interface copy for my tool:
  Archetype: [ARCHETYPE]
  Voice: [ADJ1, ADJ2, ADJ3]
  Hero: [YOUR HERO COPY]
  CTA: [YOUR CTA TEXT]
  Error: [YOUR ERROR MESSAGE]
  Empty state: [EMPTY STATE TEXT]
  Uncertain response: [I-DON'T-KNOW TEXT]
  Audit voice, claim, and failure state alignment.
  Rewrite every MISALIGNED element."

beats.narration_text: Build an Interface-Brand Alignment Checker with Claude. This is Liam, in for Bear — build it with Claude, then take it apart.
